disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pain onset approximately 45 minutes after a high-fat meal, radiating from right upper abdomen to the right shoulder blade
trigger_question_examples: Did the pain start after eating, and does it spread anywhere?
When did the pain start in relation to your meal?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, it was maybe 45 minutes after I finished a big fatty steak dinner, and it shot right into my shoulder blade.

trigger_question_examples: Have you noticed any fever, chills, or shivering?
Do you feel hot or feverish at all?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I don't think I've had any fever or chills.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No yellowing of the eyes or skin noticed
trigger_question_examples: Have you or anyone noticed any yellowing of your eyes or skin?
Any change in the colour of your urine or stools?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, nothing like that, my spouse hasn't noticed anything either.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pain is beginning to ease off after about 3 hours, from severe (8/10) toward moderate
trigger_question_examples: How is the pain now compared to when it started?
Is the pain settling, staying the same, or getting worse?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's actually starting to settle a bit now, it was much worse an hour or two ago.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Since the pain is settling, can I just stay home and see the GP next week?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Should validate that improving pain without fever/jaundice is reassuring but should still recommend prompt same-day/next-day in-person review (since exam and ultrasound cannot be done via telehealth) and give explicit safety-netting for red flags rather than simply agreeing to a routine GP visit next week without any interim safety-net.
